queries: personas CLI subcommand install list search
persona source community built-in status field
github fork pull request creation via gh cli

build_from.reason: Hosts the `personas` cobra command tree (`install`, `list`, `search`, `remove`, `test`, `upgrade`) built by `newPersonasCmd()`. A new `submit` subcommand slots in the same way: a `newPersonasSubmitCmd()` function registered via `cmd.AddCommand(...)` alongside the existing six (line 106-113).
build_from.suggested_approach: Add `newPersonasSubmitCmd()` following the existing subcommand pattern (cobra.Command with Args/RunE), reuse `commpersonas.TestPersona(name, personasFixtureRunner)` (already used by `newPersonasTestCmd()` at line 298-328) as the local fixture gate before any GitHub call, then shell out to `gh` (fork + create branch + commit + `gh pr create`) since the repo has no existing `gh` CLI invocation and the existing `internal/ghaction.Client` is a token-based REST client built for the GitHub Action / --auto-fix bot flow, not an end-user fork-on-their-own-account flow.

Built for a single configured Token against one repo, not a fork-from-the-user's-own-account flow â€” `personas submit` likely needs `gh` CLI (fork + push + `gh pr create`) instead of extending this client, since forking under an arbitrary user's account is not something a fixed bot Token can do.

existing_patterns.name: Cobra subcommand registration
existing_patterns.description: Each `personas` verb is a `newPersonas<Verb>Cmd() *cobra.Command` function added via `cmd.AddCommand(...)` in `newPersonasCmd()`; `RunE` closures call into `internal/personas` package functions and format output with `fmt.Fprintf` to `cmd.OutOrStdout()`/`cmd.ErrOrStderr()`
existing_patterns.follow_for: The new `submit` subcommand's cobra wiring, argument validation (`usageArgs(cobra.ExactArgs(1))`), and output formatting

existing_patterns.name: Injectable seams for testability
existing_patterns.description: Package-level vars (`personasDir`, `personasClient`, `personasFixtureRunner`) hold the production implementation but are swapped in tests via `withFixtureRunner`-style helpers, keeping CLI tests free of real network/filesystem calls
existing_patterns.follow_for: Any new external dependency `submit` introduces (a `gh` CLI invoker, a GitHub client) should be an injectable package var/interface so submit's tests can stub it out rather than shelling out for real

existing_patterns.name: FixtureRunner interface + local-only render check
existing_patterns.description: `TemplateFixtureRunner.RunFixture` renders the persona template against its committed patch fixture and asserts no unrendered `{{ }}` markers remain â€” zero network, zero LLM calls
existing_patterns.follow_for: The exact gate `personas submit` must run before opening a PR (AC1: "a failing fixture blocks submission with a clear error")

existing_patterns.name: GitHub REST client with redaction, retry, and typed APIError
existing_patterns.description: `internal/ghaction.Client` wraps GitHub's REST API with exponential backoff on 5xx/429, secret redaction on outgoing title/body, and a typed `*APIError` for non-retriable failures
existing_patterns.follow_for: Reference implementation quality bar (retry/redaction/error typing) if `submit` ends up needing any direct REST call rather than shelling out to `gh` entirely

existing_patterns.name: Persona name validation and safe path resolution
existing_patterns.description: `internal/personas/paths.go` centralizes name validation (`validatePersonaName`, regex `[a-zA-Z0-9_/-]`, rejects `..`/absolute paths) and maps a name to its on-disk path under a directory with a relative-path guard; `internal/registry/persona.go:validateName` mirrors the same rules for the resolver
existing_patterns.follow_for: `personas submit <name>` must validate `name` and resolve its `.yaml` (and optional `.md`) path before any fork/PR work; reuse these helpers rather than reimplementing traversal guards

existing_patterns.name: Atomic file writes with symlink/rollback guards
existing_patterns.description: `internal/personas/unit.go:writeFileAtomic` stages to a sibling temp file, refuses symlinked destinations, chmods 0600, and renames into place; `internal/atomicfs/atomic.go:WriteFileAtomic` provides the same pattern cross-package for 0644 artifacts
existing_patterns.follow_for: If `submit` persists a local `submitted` status marker or attribution file, use one of these atomic writers instead of a bare `os.WriteFile`; both already handle temp-file cleanup and symlink TOCTOU

existing_patterns.name: CLI precondition-check pattern
existing_patterns.description: `skill/SKILL.md` documents the `atcr` skill's pre-flight checks: the binary must be on PATH, the cwd must be a git work tree, and `gh` must be authenticated for PR resolution, with clear halt messages before any expensive work
existing_patterns.follow_for: Mirror this pattern for `personas submit`: check `gh` presence and `gh auth status` (or equivalent) before forking, with an actionable error message if `gh` is missing or unauthenticated

reusable_components.name: TestPersona / TemplateFixtureRunner
reusable_components.usage_example: outcome, err := commpersonas.TestPersona(name, personasFixtureRunner); if err != nil || outcome.Passed != outcome.Total { return fmt.Errorf("fixture gate failed for %q", name) }

reusable_components.name: validatePersonaName / personaPath
reusable_components.usage_example: yamlPath, err := commpersonas.PersonasDir(); then use internal helpers or reuse PersonasDir + validatePersonaName to locate the unit

reusable_components.name: writeFileAtomic
reusable_components.usage_example: use writeFileAtomic(markerPath, data) if submit persists any local status/attribution marker

architecture_notes: No existing code shells out to the `gh` CLI (`grep -rn "exec.Command(\"gh\"" --include="*.go"` returns nothing); `personas submit` would be the first such integration point in the codebase, so its `gh` invocation wrapper should be an injectable seam (interface or package var) matching the existing `personasClient`/`personasFixtureRunner` testability pattern rather than a bare `exec.Command` call inline in the cobra RunE.

The existing `internal/ghaction.Client` is architecturally a poor fit for `personas submit`: it authenticates with one fixed bot Token against one configured repo (the --auto-fix GitHub Action flow, Epic 17.0), whereas `submit` needs to fork `samestrin/atcr` under the *invoking user's own* GitHub identity and open a PR from their fork â€” a flow `gh` CLI (`gh repo fork`, `gh pr create`) handles natively via the user's local `gh auth` session, with no bot token plumbing required.

`Source` (`internal/personas/list.go:22`, comment says "built-in"|"community" but `listProject` at line 113 also sets it to "project") is a provenance axis, not a closed two-value field. AC2's `submitted` status must NOT be added as another `Source` value â€” it needs its own field/concept (e.g., a status marker file, a YAML frontmatter key, or a separate manifest) so `Source` keeps meaning only resolver provenance, per the epic's terminology-collision resolution.

The contribution checklist in `docs/personas-authoring.md` (line 162) already documents everything a human contributor manually verifies pre-PR (YAML validity, fixture presence/pass, no secrets, index entry consistency); `personas submit`'s automated local gate is effectively an automated subset of this checklist (specifically the fixture-pass item) and AC4's docs update should cross-reference rather than duplicate it.

`personas submit <name>` should validate `name` through the existing `internal/personas/paths.go:validatePersonaName` guard (or the equivalent `internal/registry/persona.go:validateName`) before resolving the on-disk `.yaml`/`.md` unit. This avoids reimplementing path-traversal protection and keeps the submit path consistent with install/search/remove.

If a local `submitted` status marker or attribution file is introduced, persist it with the existing atomic-write helper (`internal/personas/unit.go:writeFileAtomic` for 0600 persona-adjacent files, or `internal/atomicfs/atomic.go:WriteFileAtomic` for 0644 artifacts), never with a bare `os.WriteFile`. Keep the marker outside the vetted `personas/community/` tree so graduation remains a maintainer review action, not an automated copy.

Precondition checking for `gh` should follow the pattern in `skill/SKILL.md` (used by the atcr skill for PR resolution): verify the binary is on PATH and authenticated, and halt with a clear, actionable error before any fork/branch/commit work begins. This directly addresses the risk-mitigation note in plan.md.
